aliases.canonical: modest proposal for "good enough" authentication via your own url: oauth + twitter (aka twitterauth) + rel-me bidirectional identity consolidation. works for all users who enter their site in their twitter "more info url" setting, and put a rel="me" hyperlink from their site to their twitter - easier than having to write/maintain two invisible link rel="openid.*" tags to their openid server/delegate urls (and having to learn what those mean/are). - tantek

aliases.alias: modest proposal for quot good enough quot authentication via your own url oauth twitter aka twitterauth rel-me bidirectional identity consolidation. works for all users who enter their site in their twitter quot more info url quot setting and put a rel quot me quot hyperlink from their site to their twitter - easier than having to write maintain two invisible link rel quot openid. quot tags to their openid server delegate urls and having to learn what those mean are . - tantek

facts.text: modest proposal for "good enough" authentication via your own URL: OAuth + Twitter (AKA TwitterAuth) + rel-me bidirectional identity consolidation.

works for all users who enter their site in their Twitter "More Info URL" setting, and put a rel="me" hyperlink from their site to their Twitter - easier than having to write/maintain two invisible link rel="openid.*" tags to their OpenID server/delegate URLs (and having to learn what those mean/are).
- Tantek tantek.com Search ← → modest proposal for "good enough" authentication via your own URL: OAuth + Twitter (AKA TwitterAuth) + rel-me bidirectional identity consolidation.
